text: Science has a clearer story about p hacking than social media does. P hacking is manipulating data to achieve statistically significant results. Researchers might change data points or test conditions to find that magical p-value. Studies suggest that nearly 30% of research may have p hacking. This compromises trust in science. Accurate reporting matters. Stay informed. Follow for more insights on science.
